findings.title: Emergency exits permanently invalidate LinearPool virtual-supply accounting, yet the pool auto-resumes normal operation after the buffer period

best_agent_match.title: Emergency exits can leave the pool in an invalid state that automatically becomes live again

findings.title: `getRate()` can expose transient join/exit state as a manipulable on-chain rate oracle

best_agent_match.title: Unprotected `getRate()` can observe transient join/exit state and act as a manipulable rate oracle

findings.title: AaveLinearPool does not enforce that the wrapped token actually matches the Aave rate source it uses for pricing

best_agent_match.title: AaveLinearPool over-trusts wrapper metadata and can overvalue an incompatible wrapped token

rejected_candidates.title: Wrapped Token Rate Oracle Manipulation
rejected_candidates.reason: `getReserveNormalizedIncome` is an Aave accrual index, not a spot price that an attacker can realistically pump and revert with a simple same-block deposit/withdraw arbitrage as described.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner Can Exploit Pending Fees via setTargets
rejected_candidates.reason: `setTargets` already requires the current main balance to be within both the current targets and the proposed new targets, which prevents changing target ranges while fees are pending.

rejected_candidates.title: Initialization Front-Running Vulnerability
rejected_candidates.reason: `initialize()` is intentionally public and only performs the one-time self-join that mints preminted BPT to the pool itself; the report does not show theft, lockup, or other protocol-level harm from a third party calling it first.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential Division by Zero in LinearMath._fromNominal
rejected_candidates.reason: `BasePool` caps `swapFeePercentage` at 10%, so `FixedPoint.ONE - fee` cannot approach zero in a way that causes the claimed failure mode.

rejected_candidates.title: Query Functions Allow Arbitrary Caller
rejected_candidates.reason: `queryJoin` and `queryExit` are intentionally external non-view quote helpers that use a revert-based self-call pattern; they do not commit state and the report does not establish a concrete harmful side effect.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Access Control on setSwapFeePercentage
rejected_candidates.reason: Access control is not missing: `setSwapFeePercentage` is protected by `authenticate` through the Vault Authorizer by design. A compromised admin is a governance threat model, not a contract bug here.
